content:
Member-only story

The job market for junior developers is brutal right now. And honestly? AI is a big reason why.

3 min read
Just now

--

Tools like Claude Code, Copilot, and Cursor can churn out boilerplate, CRUD operations, and standard algorithms faster than any human. The tasks that used to give junior devs their start? AI handles them in seconds.
But here’s the twist, job boards are still full of software engineering roles. So what’s going on?

PS: Generated with Claude
The new problem AI created
AI didn’t just take jobs. It created a mess.
Codebases are being flooded with unoptimized, context-blind, AI-generated code, and someone has to clean it up. That someone is a senior engineer. Companies aren’t hiring juniors to ship features anymore. They’re hiring senior and mid-level engineers to fix what the AI got wrong.
If you’re a mid-level developer trying to stay relevant, this is your moment, but only if you’re genuinely better than the AI. Not just faster at prompting it.
The copy-paste trap
Here’s a real example that perfectly shows the problem.
